title: Lusion — Crafted with motion + matter
description: T662 / T74 cycle 4: structural mirror of lusion.co — htmlburger-listed creative-tech studio with WebGL-dominant landing. Fourth animated rebuild. Same vertical as sphericalwaves.com (3D + WebGL), so this is the decisive test for Canvas3D dedup: 1-of-3 from sphericalwaves bumps to 2-of-4 if Lusion surfaces it (it will), which would promote Canvas3D from LOW to MED for the animated vertical.

sections.title: Crafted with motion and matter
sections.lede: A small team of designers, developers, and 3D artists building interactive experiences that feel like physical things. We do production from start to finish — concept, design, code, launch.

sections.title: GAP-T662-6 (re-surface) — Canvas3D NOW 2-of-4 animated
body: Lusion's hero is a full-bleed WebGL canvas — a generative liquid-metal surface that responds to cursor position with ray-marched reflections. Same Canvas3D need as sphericalwaves (cycle 2). Dedup count: 2-of-4 animated surfaces → bump Canvas3D from LOW to MED priority for animated rebuilds.

Both Lusion and sphericalwaves are 3D-vertical studios; if a future animated rebuild of a non-3D studio also surfaces Canvas3D (unlikely — typography portfolios don't ship WebGL), bump to HIGH. Until then, Canvas3D is the 3D-vertical-specific gap and ships only when the owner picks a Canvas3D-needing project.

Proposed shape recap: Canvas3D { scene_module: String, audio_src: Option<String>, fallback_poster: CmsImage (REQUIRED — no-JS / prefers-reduced-motion baseline), controls: CanvasControls, intensity: f32 (0..=1) }. Same-origin scene_module + SRI required per AVP-2.

sections.title: GAP-T662-8 (re-surface) — ScrollRevealText NOW 4-of-4 animated
body: Every section heading on Lusion has the per-character scroll-reveal pattern. 4-of-4 animated surfaces — every animated rebuild needs it. ScrollRevealText is the locked-HIGH animated primitive; this is the one to ship in loom-cms-render next.
Bump confidence to MAXIMUM: 4-of-4 means 'every interactive-design / motion site needs this'. Implementation priority equivalent to LogoWall for marketing rebuilds (4 surfaces, locked-priority).

sections.title: Who we are
body: Eight people, two cities. Roughly half design + concept, half engineering. Most projects pair a designer + developer + 3D artist from kickoff. We don't subcontract code; we don't subcontract design.
We've built for cultural institutions (Maison Margiela, Rick Owens), independent musicians, motion studios, and a few brands. We turn down anything we wouldn't want to show our friends.

sections.body: We're suspicious of any brief that tells us the answer before it tells us the problem. The interesting work is in the disagreement.

sections.title: GAP-T662-9 (re-surface) — Quote NOW 7-of-7 universal
body: Quote variant fires AGAIN. Tally across both rebuild families: marketing (Stripe + Linear + Vercel + NYT + Atlantic) + animated (aristidebenoist + Lusion) = 7-of-7 sites needing Quote. (marcodeluca + sphericalwaves were earlier animated rebuilds; let me re-check — actually marcodeluca + sphericalwaves did NOT use Quote so the count is 7-of-9 total rebuilds, NOT 7-of-7.) Either way: most universal CmsSection primitive by a wide margin. T660 P2 implementation continues to pay back across every vertical.

items.value: Released a 200-line vertex-shader util crate to GitHub. MIT.
MM-archive site shipped. Case study + recorded process walkthrough up.
Speaking at FWD Conf Berlin on 'WebGL as physical-design tool'.
We pick up roughly six projects/year; current waitlist: Q4 2026.

sections.text: T662 cycle 4 (4th animated rebuild) tightens dedup table: ScrollRevealText 4-of-4 (MAX confidence — ship next in loom-cms-render). Canvas3D 2-of-4 → MED for animated 3D vertical. Quote 7-of-9 (1st-ever miss on marcodeluca+sphericalwaves) → still STRONG, still load-bearing. KineticTitle stays 2-of-4 → MED.
